In the FuelLabs/fuels-rs release v0.66.1, the project adds default gas estimation horizon in transaction builders, fixes string slice decoding, and bumps fuel-core.

Enhancements cover discovery namespace metadata, OTLP UnderscoreEscapingWithoutSuffixes strategy, OTLP scope metadata promotion, OTLP __type__/__unit__ labels, traceparent header in scrapes, UI option to disable query messages, improved UI metadata handling, and TSDB stale series tracking via prometheus_tsdb_head_stale_series. Performance improvements from internal PromQL optimizations. Bug fixes address config escaping errors, discovery shutdown race, OTLP target_info generation, PromQL NaN in topk/bottomk/limitk/limit_ratio, native counter histogram extrapolation, numerical error reduction, native histogram subquery inconsistencies, inconsistent rate/increase annotations, scraping memory corruption, TSDB panic with created-timestamp-zero-ingestion, and TSDB panic on empty-bucket native histograms.

Python fixes a race condition causing asyncio.run() to hang forever during shutdown, migrates to pyproject.toml build system from setup.py builds, logs error details when ExecuteBatchError occurs at DEBUG level, and updates setuptools min version to 77.0.1. Ruby fixes version comparison for the ruby_abi_version symbol for ruby 4 compatibility.

In the grpc/grpc repository, release v1.74.0 is the stable release of gRPC Core 1.74.0 (gee). Core changes include plumbing TCP write timestamps and metrics to OTel tracers, implementing fork support in Posix Event Engine, and fixing GRPC_ARG_HTTP2_STREAM_LOOKAHEAD_BYTES when BDP is disabled. Objective-C upgrades Protobuf to 31.1. PHP fully qualifies stdClass with global namespace, fixes PHPDoc for UnaryCall return type, and fixes typing of nullable parameters. Python fixes the gRPC Python Client not reconnecting in certain situations and fixes the docs website layout. Ruby adds rubygems support for linux-gnu and linux-musl platforms, enables EE fork support, returns nil for c functions expected to return a VALUE, removes connectivity state watch thread and fixes cancellations from spurious signals, and drops Ruby 3.0 support.

In the grpc/grpc repository, release v1.71.0 is the stable release of gRPC Core 1.71.0 (gears). It includes core changes such as fixing call attempt tracer lifetimes for retries, fixing a pick_first bug that stopped triggering connection attempts, adding support for service deprecated option in C++ codegen, and adding a SetDefaultEventEngine API. C++ changes include restricting grpc++_public_hdrs target visibility and adding a C++17 cmake option. Python drops support for Python 3.8 and adds musl-linux binary wheels on arm64. Ruby builds and tests Ruby 3.4 and builds native gems with Ruby 3.4 support.

In the grpc/grpc repository, release v1.73.0 is the stable release of gRPC Core 1.73.0 (gradient). Core changes include utilizing Abseil's synchronization on MacOS & iOS with a disable option, updating Protobuf to v31.0, adding GPR_DISABLE_ABSEIL_SYNC, passing through per-endpoint args in xds_override_host, exposing GRPC_OPENSSL_CLEANUP_TIMEOUT, fixing URI parsing of user_info in proxy settings, fixing a busy loop in EventEngine thread pool shutdown, adding a flag to build with openssl instead of boringssl, and enabling EventEngine client, listener, and dns experiments by default on all platforms. C++ marks OpenCensus and dependent APIs as deprecated. Python pins Cython to 3.1.1 and makes grpc_tools handle dot in proto paths. Ruby adds remove_unused_artifacts to opt build.

In the grpc/grpc repository, release v1.75.0 is the stable release of gRPC Core 1.75.0 (gemini). Core changes include cherry-picking Spiffe Verification, adding Typeguard to AIO stack in tests for Python, updating c-ares to 1.34.5, and fixing a pick_first bug that stopped attempting to connect. C++ implements retry metrics for OTel. Objective-C fixes a data race by adding custom getter for state property with synchronized locking. Python now depends on typing-extensions~=4.13, updates musllinux wheels to musllinux_1_2, fixes grpc.aio typehints, metadata type validation, serializer parameters, and an error in public API where ClientCallDetails.method was declared as str but called with bytes, and fixes installation on non-Mac Apple devices. Ruby marks credential object in channel and adds rubygems support for linux-gnu and linux-musl platforms.

In the valkey-io/valkey release 8.0.7, the upgrade urgency is SECURITY. Security fixes include CVE-2026-21863 (Remote DoS with malformed Valkey Cluster bus message) and CVE-2025-67733 (RESP Protocol Injection via Lua error_reply). Bug fixes include fixing ltrim should not call signalModifiedKey when no elements are removed, chained replica crash when doing dual channel replication, used_memory_dataset underflow due to miscalculated used_memory_overhead, avoiding crash during MODULE UNLOAD when ACL rules reference a module command and subcommand, server assert on ACL LOAD and resetchannels, and bug causing no response flush sometimes when IO threads are busy.

In the valkey-io/valkey release 8.1.3, the upgrade urgency is SECURITY due to fixes for CVE-2025-32023 preventing out-of-bounds write during hyperloglog operations and CVE-2025-48367 retrying accept on transient errors. Bug fixes include fixing missing response when AUTH is errored inside a transaction.

In the valkey-io/valkey release 8.1.0, the project announces the general availability of Valkey 8.1, a minor version update designed to further enhance performance, reliability, observability and usability over Valkey 8.0. This release is fully compatible with all previous Valkey releases as well as Redis OSS 7.2.4. Behavior changes include hiding input buffer data from being logged on protocol error when hide-user-data-from-log is enabled. Bug fixes address a crash in VM_GetCurrentUserName when no valid username is provided. The release also includes performance improvements such as optimizing bitcount command by using x86 SIMD instructions and embedding hash value in hash data type entries to reduce memory footprint. Cluster modifications add cluster-manual-failover-timeout configuration, improve error message reporting for invalid port in cluster meet command, and broadcast epoch ASAP when configEpoch changes. Module improvements add a new module API flag to bypass command validation to reduce processing overhead. Additional behavior changes enable TCP_NODELAY for engine initiated cluster and replication connections. Further bug fixes resolve ACL LOAD crash on a connected replica node, missing tracking-redir-broken when redirect client is closing, replica disconnection with TLS, file descriptor leak during dual channel replication abort, rax crash with keys larger than 512MB, RANDOMKEY infinite loop during CLIENT PAUSE, OOM in Lua cjson library, large-reply update in COMMANDLOG, setting TCP/TLS options for UNIX Domain Socket connections, and incorrect command rendering in valkey-cli with text output in multi/exec. Build and packaging changes check both arm64 and aarch64 for ARM architecture, cleanup lua object files on make distclean, and fix build error with CMake when using clang v19.

In the valkey-io/valkey release 8.0.5, the upgrade urgency is SECURITY. Bug fixes include fixing clients remaining blocked when reprocessing commands after certain blocking operations, a memory corruption issue in the sharded pub/sub unsubscribe logic, potential memory leak by ensuring module context is freed when aux_save2 callback writes no data, CLIENT UNBLOCK triggering unexpected errors when used on paused clients, missing NULL check on SSL_new() when creating outgoing TLS connections, incorrect casting of ping extension lengths to prevent silent packet drops, replica failover stall due to outdated config epoch, incorrect port/tls-port info in CLUSTER SLOTS/CLUSTER NODES after dynamic config change, ensuring empty error tables in Lua scripts don't crash Valkey, client tracking memory overhead calculation, handling divergent shard-id from nodes.conf and reconciling to the primary node's shard-id, and pre-size hashtables per slot when reading RDB files. Behavior changes include triggering election immediately during a forced manual failover (CLUSTER FAILOVER FORCE) to avoid delay and resetting ongoing election state when initiating a new manual failover. Logging and tooling improvements add support to drop all cluster packets and improve log clarity in failover auth denial message. Security fixes include CVE-2025-27151 checking length of AOF file name in valkey-check-aof and rejecting paths longer than PATH_MAX.

the JWT middleware is removed from core due to CVE-2024-51744

retrieved_facts_preview: In the labstack/echo release v4.13.0, the JWT middleware is removed from core due to CVE-2024-51744. Users are directed to labstack/echo-jwt. The release includes struct alignment optimization, maintaining backwards compatibility for map[string]interface{} binding, adding Go 1.23 to CI, improving MultipartForm test, adding support for multipart multi files binding, adding a TemplateRenderer struct, refactoring TestBasicAuth, removing a broken header, fixing bind body when content-length is -1, and making CORS middleware compile allowOrigin regexp at creation.

In the labstack/echo release v4.10.0, the JWT middleware is deprecated and moved to a separate repository. The minimum Go version is bumped to 1.17. Dependencies are updated, including x/text to 0.3.8. A helper interface for ProxyBalancer is added, and middleware.CreateExtractors is exposed. func(Context) error is refactored to HandlerFunc. HTTPError.WithInternal is added. io/ioutil usage is replaced. Staticcheck is added to CI. Relative path determination is replaced with stdlib. Square brackets are removed from IPv6 addresses in XFF. Additional configuration options for RequestLogger and Logger middleware are added. Route is added to request log. GitHub Workflows security is hardened. govulncheck is added to CI. e.Routes() is refactored and e.OnAddRouteHandler callback is introduced.

In the labstack/echo release v4.10.1, dependencies are upgraded due to a golang.org/x/net vulnerability. The new JWT repository is added to the README. ctx.path returns an empty string if there is no registered path. A context timeout middleware is added. The link to jaegertracing is updated.

In the labstack/echo release v4.14.0, middleware.Logger() is deprecated in favor of middleware.RequestLogger() or middleware.RequestLoggerWithConfig(), which use the standard library's slog logger. The default output format changes from JSON to the standard slog logger settings. Dependencies are updated. reflect.TypeFor is used. Go 1.25 is used in CI. context.go is modernized by replacing interface{} with any. Typos in SetParamValues and ContextTimeout middleware comments are fixed. BasicAuth middleware is improved with strings.Cut and RFC compliance. A duplicate plus operator in router backtracking logic is fixed. Custom private IP range check is replaced with net.IP.IsPrivate. Proxy connection is ensured to be closed in proxyRaw function. Logger middleware json string escaping and deprecation are handled. golang.org/x/* deps are updated.

In the labstack/echo release v4.11.2, golang.org/x/net is bumped to prevent CVE-2023-39325 / CVE-2023-44487 HTTP/2 Rapid Reset Attack. A randomString bias is fixed, and CSRF/RequestID middleware switch from math/random to crypto/random. Unused context in body_limit.go is deleted. Go 1.21 is used in CI. Some typos are fixed. CORS middleware is allowed to send Access-Control-Max-Age: 0. Dependencies are bumped.

There is a much anticipated unified_log for macOS, which is the replacement for asl and uses the current Apple APIs. Additionally, several tables have improved their cross-platform support. New features include adding a denylist mechanism to distributed queries. Table changes include adding cgroup_path column to processes table on Linux, adding firmware_type column to platform_info table on Windows, adding unified_log table for macOS (UAL), porting memory_devices table to Windows, porting platform_info table to M1 Macs, restoring macOS kernel_panics table on modern macOS, updating battery table on macOS M1 with correct raw battery max and current capacity, updating mdfind query timeout to 30 seconds, updating macOS password_policy table to use -1 as sentinel value for uid column, updating parsing of authorized_keys file, and updating the registry table to be case insensitive for key. Under the hood improvements include adding a mechanism to reduce memory retained on Linux, adding denylist mechanism to distributed queries, adding table spec support for COLLATE NOCASE, improving Pidfile handling, preventing the audit event system from using too much memory, and using full pathnames while creating an archive in carves. Bug fixes include fixing GetMemorySize for Windows memory_devices table, fixing tpm_info bug where values were out of date, fixing a crash when parsing ATC config with no columns, and fixing bug in GetHomeDirectories filesystem function. Documentation includes adding core to the type column description of osquery_extensions schema, adding documentation about 3rd-party dependency security, adding example for hostname form in curl_certificate table, adding info on how to use GTEST_FILTER on windows, changelog 5.4.0, describing user-context-related caveat for screenlock table, updating schema for process_open_sockets.state, and updating schema to reflect platform_info columns not available in Windows.

Under the hood improvements include adding version collations to column definitions, adding support for additional collations in column definitions, adding version collate functions, adding cache and throttling for certificates, keychain_acls, and keychain_items tables to reduce the occurrence of keychain corruption due to broken macOS APIs, and marking pid column as additional instead of index in process_open_sockets. Bug fixes include adding stricter checks to JSON parsing, fixing signed/unsigned mismatch in powershell_events, fixing a crash in firefox_addons, and correcting the aws_sts_region behavior. Documentation includes updating building.md prereqs for Windows, correcting link to a PR in the 4.7.0 changelog, calling out in the CHANGELOG the format changes of the status logs decorations, removing some duplicated lines from 5.8.1 changelog, fixing typo in table specs, keychain cache and throttling documentation, and changelog 5.10.2. Table changes include new tables: es_process_file_events for macOS Endpoint Security based FIM, password_policy table for macOS, and windows_update_history. Other table changes include adding memory_available to linux memory_info table, porting the cpu_info table to linux, removing the lldp_neighbors table, updating deb_packages table to not display arch info in the package name, updating hardware_model in the system_info table on Apple M1 machines to report correctly, and updating shared_resources table to add type names and fix type/maximum_allowed handling. Under the hood improvements include expanding env vars before trying to enumerate crashes in windows_crashes table, implementing a split and trim function using std::string_view, improving scheduled query denylisting and scheduler shutdown, preventing CLI_FLAGs to be set via config, and removing unnecessary string copy. Bug fixes include adding linwin to list of supported PLATFORM_DIRS, fixing AWS certificate verification failing on all services, fixing MBCS support on Windows, fixing local_timezone column in the time table on Windows, fixing system_info table to support unicode on Windows, fixing multiple Yara leaks, fixing std::bad_alloc on pci_devices on Apple Silicon macs, fixing tables spec files to specify linux and not posix, and fixing thrift server shutting down when dropping privileges. prototype_gold_evidence.answer_text: Osquery 5.2.3 is a security update that focuses on updating some third-party libraries which contained CVEs that could affect osquery. Additionally some other third-party libraries and tables have been dropped, since they were not maintained or considered safe anymore.
Big shoutout for the Windows Arm port! New Features: Add support for Windows on Arm, logger: Add new string_batch request type to compliment existing string type.

retrieved_facts_preview: Episode: In the osquery/osquery release 5.2.3, osquery is a security update that focuses on updating some third-party libraries which contained CVEs that could affect osquery. Additionally, some other third-party libraries and tables have been dropped since they were not maintained or considered safe anymore. Deprecation notices include removing the shortcut_files table, removing the ssdeep library and its support in the hash table, and removing the libelfin library and elf parsing tables. Hardening includes updating OpenSSL from version 1.1.1l to 1.1.1n, updating zlib from v1.2.11 to v1.2.12, updating librpm to 4.17.0, and updating expat from version 2.2.10 to 2.4.7. | valid_at=2026-06-06T18:47:15.886991Z

Episode: In the osquery/osquery release 5.8.2, this is a hotfix for how osquery's COM security initialization works. Bug fixes include fixing empty batch result set reporting, fixing COM security initialization by setting COM security per interface level, and fixing username field in managed_policy table. Documentation includes CHANGELOG 5.8.1. Build changes include test: do not always expect a row from the secureboot table, cmake: only link against the experiments loader when needed, tests: fix some tests becoming osquery shells, test: fix SystemdUnitsTest missing the unit_file_state column, and tests: do not always build root tests on Linux. | valid_at=2026-06-06T19:28:44.503005Z

Episode: In the osquery/osquery release 5.0.1, osquery 5.0 is announced as a tremendously exciting release. Installation now uses /opt/osquery on macOS and Linux for better portability. The default macOS installation uses an application bundle to support entitlement-based features. Endpoint Security APIs are used for various event-based tables on macOS. An osquery-organization macOS code signing certificate is now used. Breaking changes include installation path changes from /usr/local to /opt/osquery, macOS codesigning now done through the Osquery Foundation account, the need to update macOS full disk permission profiles, removal of the deprecated blacklist key from configuration, and changed search semantics on the augeas table. Table changes include adding secureboot for Linux and Windows, tpm_info for Windows, fixing osquery_info build_platform on Linux, supporting pid_with_namespace in more tables, updating augeas to use native pattern matching (breaking), updating chrome_extensions to include Edge and EdgeBeta, updating disk_encryption to support QueryContext, updating last to include utmp type name column, updating sudoers to support newer include syntax, and updating user_ssh_keys to detect encryption of ed25519 keys. Under the hood improvements include adding ruby namespace to thrift definition, always initializing variable change in PerformanceChange, removing deprecated blacklist key, using total_size within watchdog on Windows, supporting AF_PACKET sockets reporting on Linux, and socket_events improvements in Linux audit system. Bug fixes include adding case sensitive pragma to the pragma/actions authorizer allow list, adding feature to skip denylist for event-based queries, changing logger_mode flag to be correctly interpreted as octal, preventing osquery from creating multiple copies of the extension running at once, fixing Linux audit rule removal upon osquery exit, fixing broadcasting empty logs to logger plugins, fixing issues applying ACLs during chocolatey deployment, and fixing a memory issue. | valid_at=2026-06-06T18:25:06.201740Z
Episode: In the osquery/osquery release 5.2.0, the release note directs users to use version 5.2.2 instead. | valid_at=2026-06-06T18:37:59.153184Z
Episode: In the osquery/osquery release 5.2.1, the release note directs users to use version 5.2.2 instead. | valid_at=2026-06-06T18:39:06.397098Z

retrieved_facts_preview: Episode: In the nomic-ai/gpt4all release v3.8.0, native DeepSeek-R1-Distill support is added with several model variants available on the downloads page, reasoning displayed similarly to the Reasoner model, the DeepSeek-R1 Qwen pretokenizer supported, and a GPT4All-compatible prompt template configured by default. The chat templating parser is completely replaced with one having much better compatibility with common models. Code interpreter fixes address an issue preventing logging a single string in v3.7.0 and the UI freezing while the code interpreter runs. Markdown support is added, and CUDA/Metal device option for embeddings is supported. Fixes include embedding tokenization after #2310, a crash when loading certain models with 'code' in their name, an embedding crash with large chunk sizes after #2310, inability to load models with non-ASCII path on Windows, non-fatal DLL errors on Windows with CUDA, LocalDocs fixes (always using requested number of snippets even if better matches in unselected collections, checking for deleted files on startup), CUDA PTX errors with some builds, blank device in UI after model switch and improved usage stats, using CPU instead of CUDA backend when GPU loading fails the first time, and a crash when sending a message greater than n_ctx tokens after #1970. | valid_at=2026-06-06T18:35:14.046188Z

Episode: In the open-webui/open-webui release v0.6.41, sign-in rate limiting is implemented to protect against brute force attacks, limiting login attempts to 15 per 3-minute window per email address using Redis with automatic fallback to in-memory storage. Administrators can now globally disable the folders feature and control user-level folder permissions. Group channels are introduced as a new channel type enabling membership-based collaboration spaces with public or private visibility, automatic member inclusion from specified user groups, member role tracking, and post-creation member management. Direct Message channels are introduced with a dedicated channel type selector and multi-user member selection interface. Direct Message channels now support a complete user-to-user messaging system with member-based access control, automatic deduplication for one-on-one conversations, optional channel naming, and distinct visual presentation using participant avatars. Users can now hide Direct Message channels from their sidebar while preserving message history, with automatic reactivation when new messages arrive. success_candidates.query_text: Across the Aider-AI/aider releases, how does the watch files feature in v0.67.0 differ from the watch files improvements in v0.69.0?
success_candidates.expected_answer: v0.67.0 introduces AI! comments to trigger aider, while v0.69.0 adds AI? comments for asking questions and extends watching to all files.

prototype_gold_evidence.answer_text: One-liner '# ...' or '// ...' comments that start or end with 'AI' are instructions to aider. When aider sees 'AI!' it reads and follows all the instructions in AI comments.
Use '# ... AI?' comments to trigger aider and ask questions about your code. Now watches *all* files, not just certain source files.

retrieved_facts_preview: In the Aider-AI/aider release v0.67.0, aider can be used in an IDE or editor by running 'aider --watch-files', which watches for instructions added to source files. One-liner '# ...' or '// ...' comments that start or end with 'AI' are instructions to aider; when aider sees 'AI!' it reads and follows all instructions in AI comments. Support for new Amazon Bedrock Nova models is added. When /run or /test have non-zero exit codes, 'Fix that' is pre-filled into the next message prompt. /diff now invokes 'git diff' to use the preferred diff tool. Ctrl-Z support for process suspension is added. The spinner falls back to ASCII art if fancy symbols throw unicode errors. --read now expands '~' home dirs. Exception capture in analytics is enabled, and Aider wrote 61% of the code in this release.

In the Aider-AI/aider release v0.69.0, watch files improvements include using '# ... AI?' comments to trigger aider and ask questions about code, watching all files instead of just certain source files, and using '# AI comments', '// AI comments', or '-- AI comments' to give aider instructions in any text file. Full support for Gemini Flash 2.0 Exp is added with shortcuts 'aider --model flash' or 'aider --model gemini/gemini-2.0-flash-exp'. A new --multiline flag and /multiline-mode command make ENTER a soft newline and META-ENTER send the message. /copy-context <instructions> now takes optional instructions when copying code context to the clipboard. Clipboard error handling is improved with helpful requirements install info. 5% of users are asked to opt-in to analytics. /voice now lets you edit the transcribed text before sending. Auto-complete is disabled in Y/N prompts, and Aider wrote 68% of the code in this release.

success_candidates.query_text: In the Aider-AI/aider release window, order the following release-content states from earliest to latest: (1) a release that adds support for GPT-5 models, (2) a release that adds support for GPT 4.1, mini and nano, (3) a release that adds full support for o3-mini with a --reasoning-effort argument.

Support for GPT 4.1, mini and nano.
Added support for all GPT-5 models.

retrieved_facts_preview: In the Aider-AI/aider release v0.73.0, full support for o3-mini is added with 'aider --model o3-mini'. A new --reasoning-effort argument accepts low, medium, or high. Handling of context window size limits is improved with better messaging and Ollama-specific guidance. Support for removing model-specific reasoning tags from responses with 'remove_reasoning: tagname' model setting is added. Parent directories are auto-created when creating new files. Support for R1 free on OpenRouter is added with '--model openrouter/deepseek/deepseek-r1:free'. User/assistant turn order is enforced to avoid R1 errors. Model name matching is case-insensitive while preserving original case. Hardening against user/assistant turn order problems is applied. Model metadata for 'openrouter/deepseek/deepseek-r1' is fixed, and Aider wrote 69% of the code in this release.

In the Aider-AI/aider release v0.82.0, support for GPT 4.1, mini and nano is added. Support for using architect mode with Gemini 2.5 Pro is improved. Support is added for xai/grok-3-beta, xai/grok-3-mini-beta, openrouter/x-ai/grok-3-beta, openrouter/x-ai/grok-3-mini-beta, openrouter/openrouter/optimus-alpha, grok-3-fast-beta, and grok-3-mini-fast-beta models. A new 'patch' edit format for OpenAI's GPT-4.1 model is added, along with new 'editor-diff', 'editor-whole', and 'editor-diff-fenced' edit formats. A bugfix automatically selects the best edit format to use in architect mode. Aliases 'grok3' for xai/grok-3-beta and 'optimus' for openrouter/openrouter/optimus-alpha are added. URL extraction from error messages is fixed. Files can be added by full path even if a file with the same basename is already in the chat. Quoting of values containing '#' in the sample aider.conf.yml is fixed. Support for Fireworks AI model 'deepseek-v3-0324' is added, and Aider wrote 92% of the code in this release.

In the Aider-AI/aider release v0.86.0, support for all GPT-5 models is added. Support for Grok-4 via xai/grok-4 and openrouter/x-ai/grok-4 model names is added. Support for gemini/gemini-2.5-flash-lite-preview-06-17 model is added. /clear now prints 'All chat history cleared.' so you know it worked. /undo output now shows only the first line of each commit message, making it easier to read. Support for openrouter/moonshotai/kimi-k2 model is added. Model announcements are displayed with no-arg /model command. An issue where new settings for an existing model didn't replace the old ones is fixed. Analytics is fixed to support the latest PostHog SDK event-capture API. Dependencies are bumped to pick up latest litellm==1.75.0, and Aider wrote 88% of the code in this release.

It updates project settings, GitHub Actions, tests, and formatting for newer Xcodes, updates use of URLRequest.headers in URLEncoding, updates the iOS example app and various documentation, and fixes a double lock crash in AuthenticationInterceptor caused by synchronous refresh implementations, a crash when requesting file:// URLs by producing a validation error, missing rethrow of error when writing temporary multipart files fails, and Objective-C module conflicts in Xcode 12 by removing Obj-C module support.

In the Alamofire/Alamofire release 5.4.0 (URLs and File Downloads), the release adds URLResponseSerializer and attendant convenience methods so downloads can produce a non-optional URL, and updates handling of file:// URLs, removing the error added in 5.3.0 and adding support for DownloadRequest.

success_candidates.query_text: In the Alamofire/Alamofire release window, order the following release-content states from earliest to latest: (1) a release that adds request body compression support, (2) a release that adds Combine support with publishers, (3) a release that adds experimental WebSocketRequest, (4) a release that adds full Swift concurrency support with Sendable requirements.

retrieved_facts_preview: In the Alamofire/Alamofire release 5.2.0 (Combine and AuthenticationInterceptor), the release adds Combine support with DataResponsePublisher, DownloadResponsePublisher, and DataStreamPublisher, and adds AuthenticationInterceptor for easier adaptation and retry of requests with credentials. It updates response handlers to take all serializer parameters, renames DisabledEvaluator to DisabledTrustEvaluator, allows Interceptor to be initialized with multiple RequestInterceptors, and fixes minor documentation issues, encoding of Decimal values in URLEncodedFormEncoder, a thread-safety issue in Session, and a memory leak in NetworkReachabilityManager.

In the Alamofire/Alamofire release 5.7.0, the release adds request body compression support (disabled by default), ArrayEncoding.custom to URLEncodedFormEncoder and URLEncoding, KeyPathEncoding to URLEncodedFormEncoder, and NilEncoding to URLEncodedFormEncoder. It updates Alamofire to require Swift 5.5+ and (macOS 10.13, iOS 11, tvOS 11, watchOS 4)+, updates flatMap to map in HTTPHeaders, updates various CI integrations, and fixes reachability API usage safety, unused bufferingPolicy in StreamOf, and various documentation typos.

In the Alamofire/Alamofire release 5.9.0, the release adds experimental WebSocketRequest as a wrapper for URLSessionWebSocketTask, a PrivacyInfo.xcprivacy file and integration with SPM, CocoaPods, and Carthage, an AlamofireDynamic target to force dynamic linking in Xcode when using SPM, and AFInfo enum with a public version value to get Alamofire's current version. It updates Alamofire to require Swift 5.7.1, restructures the project to break apart large Request.swift file, updates Empty type to be Sendable, updates .swiftformat to remove duplicate rules, and fixes platform deprecation warnings in Package.swift, Alamofire.podspec after project restructuring, and various documentation typos.

In the Alamofire/Alamofire release 5.10.0, the release adds full Swift concurrency support with Sendable requirements, requires existential any internally, updates Session.init(eventMonitors:) to allow no EventMonitors at all and fixes CompositeEventMonitor thread-safety, updates DownloadRequest.validate to read the fileSize rather than the whole file from disk, updates links to swiftlang repositories, and fixes various documentation typos, passing queue parameter to DataStream serializers, and a test for date encoding when running with a non-Gregorian calendar.

New features include a -f/--file flag for reading patterns from a file, a --colors flag for customizing output colors, a --files-without-match flag, and completion files for Bash, Fish, and PowerShell. Bug fixes address ANSI escape sequences for screen.linux, automatic stdin detection on Windows, color support in mintty and Emacs, reduced help content with -h, non-UTF-8 file names on Unix, line buffering, and error message suppression with --no-messages.

Bug fixes include making smart case less conservative, clarifying --heading/--no-heading, fixing a matching bug caused by literal optimizations, correcting behavior differences when searching symlinks, and resolving a regression from 0.3.0 that prevented patterns starting with '-' from being used with the -e/--regexp flag.

retrieved_facts_preview: In the BurntSushi/ripgrep release 0.10.0, the minimum supported Rust version now tracks the latest stable Rust. Breaking changes include altered match semantics for -w/--word-regexp. Major new features include libripgrep, PCRE2 support via -P/--pcre2, multi-line search with -U/--multiline, and a JSON output format with --json. Other new features include a --one-file-system flag, --sort and --sortr flags (deprecating --sort-files), a --crlf flag, a --trim flag, a --null-data flag, --passthru working with --replace, --line-buffered and --block-buffered flags, and a --pre-glob flag. Bug fixes address searching with non-zero context using memory maps, correct stopping when output pipe is closed, more intuitive -w/--word-regexp, improved stdin detection on Windows, and matching empty lines.

In the BurntSushi/ripgrep release 11.0.0, the versioning scheme changes from 0.x to major version 11. The minimum supported Rust version increases to 1.34.0. Breaking changes include tweaked exit status codes to match GNU grep, -uuu now equivalent to --no-ignore --hidden --binary, and removal of the avx-accel feature. Performance improvements make rg -F -f dictionary-of-literals much faster. New features include a --binary flag, a --max-columns-preview flag, Brotli and Zstd support with -z/--search-zip, a --no-ignore-dot flag, an --auto-hybrid-regex flag, exit status logic matching GNU grep, --ignore-file-case-insensitive, and other enhancements. File type filtering is added or improved for many types including Apache Thrift, ASP, Bazel, Brotli, BuildStream, bzip2, C, C++, Cython, gzip, Java, Make, Postscript, QML, Tex, XML, xz, zig, and zstd.

In the BurntSushi/ripgrep release 14.0.0, breaking changes include a modification to how -A and -B flags override -C. Build process changes involve generating shell completions and man page via a new --generate flag, dropping the asciidoc/asciidoctor dependency. Performance improvements make inner literal cases faster, speed up \b look-arounds, and improve parallel directory traversal. New features include a --hyperlink-format flag for hyperlink support, improved documentation for tty behavior, Apple silicon binaries, a --stop-on-nonmatch flag, and categorized flags in -h/--help. File type filtering is added or improved for Ada, DITA, Elixir, Fuchsia, Gentoo, Gradle, GraphQL, Markdown, Prolog, Raku, TypeScript, USD, and V. The release also contains a rewrite of its regex engine.
In the BurntSushi/ripgrep release 15.1.0, a bug fix addresses a regression with --line-buffered introduced in 15.0.0. A new feature adds a hyperlink alias for Cursor.

failure_candidates.query_text: In the hasura/graphql-engine release window, order the following release-content states from earliest to latest: (1) a release that adds an optional no_transaction flag to run_sql API, (2) a release that promotes the Relay API to stable /v1/relay, (3) a release that fixes a performance issue where large gzipped responses caused long cumulative GC sync phase pauses.

Fix a performance issue where large gzipped responses were causing long cumulative GC sync phase pauses
promote Relay API to stable /v1/relay

retrieved_facts_preview: In the hasura/graphql-engine beta release v2.46.0-beta.1, the server fixes an issue where Postgres reset JSON parameters to empty strings after a transaction, causing event trigger failures. It attempts to set Haskell runtime threads based on cgroup CPU limits for better sizing in Docker/Kubernetes, adds an optional no_transaction flag to run_sql API for executing SQL outside transaction blocks, fixes source metadata erasure on inconsistent source update, marks remote schemas as inconsistent on type conflicts, and hides internal action errors unless dev mode or admin internal errors are enabled. It also fixes the hasura_active_subscriptions metric inconsistency, increases execution time metrics upper bound to 100s, adds hasura_events_fetch_query_time and hasura_events_fetched_total metrics, and fixes metrics label for DB with non-standard URI (Cloud/Enterprise).

In the hasura/graphql-engine patch release v2.48.12, the server fixes a performance issue where large gzipped responses caused long cumulative GC sync phase pauses, and fixes duplicate event triggers firing when an event trigger was moved from one table to another. Data connectors fix a bug in the super-connector (MySQL) where queries on self-referencing tables fail with 'Relation not found' when SELECT permission filter navigates through the same self-referencing relationship, and fix update mutations on nullable columns via data connectors (Enterprise/cloud).

In the hasura/graphql-engine release v2.49.0, the server promotes the Relay API to stable /v1/relay and deprecates /v/1beta1/relay, adds an --enable-relay flag to enable/disable Relay API with different defaults per edition, improves schema memory usage, and adds HASURA_GRAPHQL_DISABLE_EVENTING env var to disable the entire eventing subsystem on OSS/single-tenant builds. Data connectors fix MariaDB update/delete mutations still failing with 'Table temp_table_xxx doesn't exist' by pinning the full CREATE/mutation/DROP lifecycle to one JDBC connection (Enterprise/cloud).

retrieved_facts_preview: In the huggingface/transformers release Vault-Gemma (based on v4.56.1), a new model Vault-Gemma is added. VaultGemma is a text-only decoder model derived from Gemma 2, dropping norms after Attention and MLP blocks and using full attention for all layers. It is a pretrained 1B parameter model with 1024 token sequence length, trained from scratch with sequence-level differential privacy (DP) using DP-SGD, providing a (ε ≤ 2.0, δ ≤ 1.1e-10)-sequence-level DP guarantee. This tag is a preview and will be included in the next minor release v4.57.0.

In the huggingface/transformers release candidate v5.0.0rc0, the first major release in five years is announced with 800 commits. Highlights include dynamic weight loading API centered around WeightConverter class, significant tokenization refactors, and many bug fixes. This is a pre-release, opt-in via pip install transformers --pre.

In the huggingface/transformers release v5.8.0, new models include DeepSeek-V4 (MoE with hybrid local+long-range attention, mHC, static hash table), Gemma 4 Assistant (speculative decoding with MTP), GraniteSpeechPlus (speech-to-text with intermediate hidden states), Granite4Vision (document data extraction), and EXAONE-4.5.
